subject: Human Resources
title: Organizational Design
description: A short lesson to help you apply Organizational Design.

hook.text: A company has 12 layers of management. Decisions take months. Competitors move in weeks.

buildup.text: Organizational design defines how a company structures teams, roles, reporting lines, and decision-making.

discovery.text: Flat structures enable speed. Hierarchical structures enable control. The right choice depends on the business.

twist.text: Most restructuring fails because it changes boxes on a chart without changing how people actually work.

climax.text: Effective design aligns structure with strategy, culture, and the kind of work the organization does.

punchline.text: Structure shapes behavior. Design it intentionally.

quiz.question: What determines the right organizational structure?
options: The company's strategy, culture, and type of work
